description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description_wiki: New Jersey is a state located in both the Mid-Atlantic and Northeastern regions of the United States.

description: neighborhood in San Diego, California, United States
US Army training during World War II in California
description_wiki: Camp Callan was a United States Army anti-aircraft artillery replacement training center that was operational during World War II. It was located on the southern West Coast of the United States, in the La Jolla community of San Diego, California.

surface_forms: University of California at San Diego

description: public university in La Jolla, California, United States
description_wiki: The University of California, San Diego (UC San Diego or UCSD) is a public land-grant research university in the La Jolla neighborhood of San Diego, California, United States.

description: United States federal government public work relief program part of the New Deal
description_wiki: The Civilian Conservation Corps (CCC) was a government work relief program that ran from 1933 to 1942 in the United States for unemployed, unmarried men ages 18–25 and eventually expanded to ages 17–28, who volunteered amid widespread unemployment.

description: census-designated place in Los Angeles County, California, United States
description_wiki: Castaic ( ) (Chumash: Kaštiq; Spanish: Castéc) is an unincorporated community and census-designated place (CDP) in northwestern Los Angeles County, California, United States.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: military base in Arizona
description_wiki: Fort Huachuca is a United States Army installation in Cochise County in southeast Arizona, approximately 15 miles (24 km) north of the border with Mexico and at the northern end of the Huachuca Mountains, adjacent to the town of Sierra Vista.

description: city in and county seat of Yavapai County, Arizona, United States
description_wiki: Prescott ( PRESS-kət) is a city in and the county seat of Yavapai County, Arizona, United States.

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

northern major peninsula of Michigan, United States of America
description_wiki: The Upper Peninsula of Michigan (also known as Upper Michigan or colloquially the U.P. or Yoop) is the northern and more elevated of the two major landmasses that make up the U.S. state of Michigan; it is separated from the Lower Peninsula by the Straits of Mackinac.

description_wiki: Fort MacArthur is a former United States Army installation in San Pedro, Los Angeles, California (now the port community of Los Angeles).

description: german-born theoretical physicist (1879–1955)
description_wiki: Albert Einstein (14 March 1879 – 18 April 1955) was a German-born theoretical physicist best known for developing the theory of relativity.

description: postgraduate center in Princeton, New Jersey, US
description_wiki: The Institute for Advanced Study (IAS) is an independent center for theoretical research and intellectual inquiry located in Princeton, New Jersey.

description_wiki: Bob's Big Boy is a casual dining restaurant chain founded by Bob Wian in Southern California in 1936, originally named Bob's Pantry.

description: city in Los Angeles County, California, United States
description_wiki: Pasadena (  PAS-ə-DEE-nə) is a city in Los Angeles County, California, United States, 10 miles (16 km) northeast of downtown Los Angeles.

description: private research university located in Pasadena, California, USA
description_wiki: The California Institute of Technology (branded as Caltech) is a private research university/institute of technology in Pasadena, California, United States.

description: neighborhood city of Los Angeles, California, United States
description_wiki: San Pedro ( san PEE-droh; Spanish for 'Saint Peter') is a neighborhood located within the South Bay and Harbor region of the city of Los Angeles, California, United States.

description: non-profit organization in the USA
description_wiki: The Los Angeles Conservancy is a historic preservation organization in Los Angeles, California, that works to document, rescue and revitalize historic buildings, places and neighborhoods in the city.

description: street in Los Angeles County, California
description_wiki: Sepulveda Boulevard is a major street and transportation corridor in the City of Los Angeles and several other cities in western Los Angeles County, California.

description: neighborhood in Los Angeles, California, United States of America
description_wiki: Beverly Glen is a neighborhood in the Santa Monica Mountains region of Los Angeles, California, located around Beverly Glen Boulevard north of Sunset Boulevard and south of Mulholland Drive.

description: neighborhood in the northern central portion of the Westside region of Los Angeles, California, USA
description_wiki: Westwood is a commercial and residential neighborhood in the northern central portion of the Westside region of the city of Los Angeles, California.

description: thoroughfare in Santa Monica, Beverly Hills, and Los Angeles, United States
description_wiki: Wilshire Boulevard ( WIL-shər) is a prominent 15.83 mi (25.48 km) boulevard in the Los Angeles area of Southern California, extending from Ocean Avenue in the city of Santa Monica east to Grand Avenue in the Financial District of downtown Los Angeles.

description: thoroughfare in Beverly Hills, West Hollywood and Los Angeles, United States
description_wiki: Sunset Boulevard is a boulevard in the central and western part of Los Angeles, California, United States, that stretches from the Pacific Coast Highway in Pacific Palisades east to Figueroa Street in downtown Los Angeles.
